text: CNN (a Warner Bros. Discovery subsidiary) sued Perplexity AI in the Southern District of New York (No. 1:26-cv-04427, filed 2026-05-28; jury demanded). The complaint alleges a two-stage harm: at the input stage, Perplexity’s "PerplexityBot" and "Perplexity-User" crawlers copy CNN content (over 17,000 works) to build an "AI-First" search index; at the output stage, Perplexity’s GenAI products (answer engine, enterprise chatbot, APIs, Comet browser) generate verbatim or substantially similar reproductions. CNN brings Copyright Act (17 U.S.C. § 101 et seq.) and Lanham Act claims, the latter including "confabulated or ‘hallucinated’ content that Perplexity falsely attributes to CNN."
ai_system_name: Perplexity answer engine + GenAI products (PerplexityBot / Perplexity-User crawlers; "AI-First" search index; RAG)
canonical_source_conflicted: CNN copyrighted works (17,000+ registrations) and CNN trademarks; 17 U.S.C. § 501 and the Lanham Act
mitigation_gap: Alleged crawling/copying of copyrighted content without licensing, and AI output that fabricates and falsely attributes content to CNN without adequate provenance or attribution controls.
reliance_or_harm: CNN alleges licensing/market harm from large-scale reproduction plus consumer confusion and brand dilution from hallucinated content falsely attributed to CNN.
